question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on EVM address lookups to perform a timing side-channel attack — measuring response time differences between existing and non-existing EVM addresses — to enumerate valid entity EVM addresses and then target those entities' gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user send a timestamp=0 parameter to /api/v1/network/supply, causing getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() to return 0 and adjustUpperBound() to return 0, resulting in a zero-range timestamp window that returns no balance records, causing consensusTimestamp=0 and EntityNotFoundException that disrupts gossip supply verification?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user send Long.MAX_VALUE as a timestamp to /api/v1/network/supply, causing getFirstDayOfMonth() to call Instant.ofEpochSecond(0, Long.MAX_VALUE) which overflows the nanosecond-to-second conversion, producing an incorrect month boundary and causing the supply query to return stale or zero data, disrupting gossip-dependent supply checks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CrudRepository.findAll() method inherited by EntityRepository (since it extends CrudRepository<Entity, Long>) to retrieve all entities from the database without pagination, causing a full table scan that exhausts DB resources and prevents gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityRepository extends CrudRepository<Entity, Long> — which exposes findById(Long id) — to enumerate entity IDs sequentially and extract entity metadata, then use that information to craft gossip transactions targeting specific entities for suppression?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition between the lazy initialization of NetworkProperties.getUnreleasedSupplyRangeBounds() (annotated @Getter(lazy=true)) and concurrent getSupply() calls, causing two threads to simultaneously compute the RangeBounds and one to pass a partially-constructed comma-separated string to getSupply(), resulting in a malformed string_to_array input and a DB exception?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSupply() performs a SUM of entity balances with COALESCE(sum(e.balance), 0) — where balance is a bigint — to cause an arithmetic overflow if the sum of balances in the unreleased supply ranges exceeds Long.MAX_VALUE, producing a negative unreleasedSupply and a corrupted releasedSupply value that misrepresents network state to gossip participants?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send a byte array alias that matches the alias of a system account (e.g., account 2, 42, or treasury accounts in the unreleased supply ranges), causing findByAlias() to return that system account's ID, and then use that ID to craft transactions that interfere with gossip-based supply tracking?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send a byte array EVM address that matches the EVM address of a system account in the unreleased supply ranges (accounts 2-2, 42-42, 44-71, etc.), causing findByEvmAddress() to return that system account's ID, and then use that ID to craft transactions that interfere with gossip-based supply tracking?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSupply() does not validate that lowerBounds and upperBounds arrays have equal length before passing them to unnest(), causing PostgreSQL to throw 'ERROR: unnest called with different-sized arrays' if the configuration is ever misconfigured, resulting in a persistent 500 error that prevents all gossip supply queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByAlias() returns Optional<Long> (entity ID only, not the full entity) to cause a TOCTOU race condition — where the entity is deleted between the findByAlias() call and the subsequent findById() call in EntityServiceImpl.lookup() — resulting in a gossip transaction being routed to a deleted entity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TOCTOU window between findByEvmAddress() returning an entity ID and the subsequent entity state fetch in EntityServiceImpl.lookup(), where the entity could be deleted in between, causing gossip transactions to be routed to a now-deleted entity and silently failing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user send a timestamp parameter with operator 'gt' (greater than) set to Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 to /api/v1/network/supply, causing getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() to return Long.MAX_VALUE after incrementing, which then overflows when compared to maxTimestamp, bypassing the minTimestamp > maxTimestamp validation and causing an incorrect supply calculation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send a byte array alias of exactly 20 bytes (EVM address length) to findByAlias(), which in the importer's EntityIdServiceImpl would be routed to findByEvmAddress() instead, but in the rest-java EntityServiceImpl is always routed to findByAlias(), causing a behavioral inconsistency that prevents gossip transactions from being correctly resolved across components?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the rest-java EntityRepository.findByAlias() uses `deleted <> true` while the importer's EntityRepository.findByAlias() uses the same predicate, but the graphql version uses `deleted is not true`, to identify entities that are visible in one service but not another, causing gossip transaction routing inconsistencies?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user send two timestamp parameters with operator 'lt' set to 1 (minimum nanosecond timestamp) to /api/v1/network/supply, causing adjustUpperBound() to return 0 after decrementing, which then causes minTimestamp (0) to equal maxTimestamp (0), resulting in a zero-range query that returns no supply data and throws EntityNotFoundException?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input size validation on the alias byte array in EntityIdAliasParameter.valueOf() — which only validates the Base32 regex pattern but not the decoded byte length — to pass an arbitrarily long decoded alias to findByAlias(), causing excessive memory usage and DB query time that degrades gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdEvmAddressParameter parsing to pass a byte array that is not exactly 20 bytes to findByEvmAddress(), causing the native query to perform a bytea comparison against a non-standard-length value that can never match any entity, silently failing gossip transaction EVM address resolution?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getSupply() uses CAST(COALESCE(sum(e.balance), 0) AS bigint) — where the inner sum is already bigint — to cause a PostgreSQL integer overflow if entity balances in the unreleased supply ranges sum to more than 9,223,372,036,854,775,807, producing an error that prevents the supply endpoint from responding and disrupts gossip supply verification?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send a byte array alias that is all-zeros (e.g., new byte[32]) to findByAlias(), which may match a default or uninitialized entity alias in the database, causing findByAlias() to return an unexpected entity ID and misroute gossip transactions to the wrong entity?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByEvmAddress(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user send a byte array EVM address that is all-zeros (new byte[20]) to findByEvmAddress(), which may match a default or uninitialized entity EVM address, causing findByEvmAddress() to return an unexpected entity ID and misroute gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getSupply(String, String)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the getSupply() query joins entity table with unnest() ranges using `e.id between ranges.min_val and ranges.max_val` — without any index hint — to cause a full sequential scan of the entity table when the ranges are very wide, resulting in a slow query that blocks gossip transaction processing threads?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that EntityRepository extends CrudRepository which exposes count() and existsById() methods — without any access control — to perform reconnaissance on the entity table size and use that information to plan a targeted DoS attack against gossip transaction processing?
